reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures both sides of the iff. `countably_compact X` encodes "every countable open cover (U : ℕ → Set X with all U i open and covering X) has a finite subcover (a Finset t with ⋃ i ∈ t, U i covering X)", exactly matching the given informal definition. `limit_point_compact_strong X` says every infinite subset U has some x with `ClusterPt x (𝓟 (U \ {x}))`, which unfolds to `𝓝 x ⊓ 𝓟 (U \ {x}) ≠ ⊥`, i.e., every neighborhood of x meets U \ {x} — the standard definition of x being a limit point of U. The coercion `Infinite U` for `U : Set X` means the subtype is infinite, i.e., U is infinite as a set. The theorem asserts the iff under the T1 hypothesis, matching the informal claim exactly. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure (∀ infinite subsets, ∃ limit point) is preserved.
